description: Defines the expected agent output for a given store context and user shopping list. Used by QA for manual validation and by the test runner for automated regression. v0.2.0 adds Restock Agent cases (TC-05 onwards) which test the agentic while-loop with tool_choice=auto.

expected.reasoning: Walmart carries all categories including hardware and grocery. Surface the full list without filtering.
FreshCo is a grocery-only store. Hardware items like a wrench are not stocked here — hide them to avoid confusion.

cases.description: Hardware store — suppress all grocery items, mute if nothing relevant remains

expected.mute_condition: If user list contained only grocery items at a hardware store, send no notification.
expected.reasoning: Home Depot carries hardware only. Surface the wrench; hide grocery items. If no relevant items remain after filtering, suppress the notification entirely.

cases.description: Gas station — delay notification based on dwell time

expected.reasoning: User is likely just fueling up. A short stop does not indicate shopping intent. Delay notification until dwell time exceeds 180 seconds — at that point the user is likely inside the store.

cases.description: Restock agent — adds stale item (bought 12 days ago), skips recent item (bought 2 days ago)
cases.how_it_works: Claude runs a while-loop with tool_choice=auto. It independently calls add_to_list or skip_item for each purchase history entry. The loop ends when Claude stops calling tools.

expected.agent_note: Added Eggs (12 days since last purchase). Skipped Milk (bought recently, 2 days ago).
expected.reasoning: Eggs are stale (>7 days) so Claude calls add_to_list. Milk is fresh (<7 days) so Claude calls skip_item. Claude autonomously decides the threshold — no code tells it the cutoff.

cases.description: Restock agent — skips item already on the current list to avoid duplicates
cases.how_it_works: Claude receives current_list alongside purchase_history. It should recognise Bread is already listed and skip it.

expected.agent_note: Skipped Bread — it is already on your list.
expected.reasoning: Even though Bread was bought 10 days ago, it is already on the current list. Claude should not create a duplicate and should use skip_item instead.

cases.description: Restock agent — empty purchase history, no tool calls expected
cases.how_it_works: With no history, Claude has nothing to reason about. It should exit the while-loop immediately (0 iterations) and return an empty result.

expected.reasoning: No purchase history means Claude has no items to evaluate. The agentic loop exits in the first iteration without calling any tools.

cases.description: Restock agent — multi-item mixed result with agent note summarising all decisions
cases.how_it_works: Claude autonomously processes all 4 items across multiple loop iterations, calling a different tool for each, then sets a final summary note.

expected.agent_note: Added Milk (9 days) and Butter (20 days). Skipped Eggs (bought yesterday) and Diapers (already on your list).
expected.reasoning: Claude makes independent decisions per item across multiple loop iterations. Milk and Butter are stale. Eggs are fresh. Diapers are already on the list. Claude writes a single summary note at the end.
